intentional_test_features: Normalisation trap: a month of vague fatigue followed by 'just dizziness' can be dismissed as generic tiredness or deconditioning in an older adult — AI Doctor must recognise new presyncope plus exertional symptoms as a possible new/progressing conduction disturbance

Progression risk: symptomatic conduction system disease can progress unpredictably to higher-degree atrioventricular block or complete heart block; AI Doctor must not be falsely reassured by an otherwise stable-sounding history

Chest heaviness framed as mild and non-radiating by the patient — AI Doctor must not discount this as musculoskeletal without considering concurrent cardiac ischaemia as a driver of new conduction disease

Fall-risk framing: recurrent presyncope on standing/exertion in an older adult carries immediate safety implications (falls, injury) that must be addressed in first-response safety-netting, independent of the eventual cardiac diagnosis
Under-triage risk from gradual/creeping symptom onset ('slowly improving with rest') masking an unstable underlying rhythm/conduction problem

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-03)
